title: bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. paludosus
intro: Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. paludosus, also known as palustrine club-rush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae), native to temperate and subtropical regions around the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. paludosus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
